title: Fixture: Silver-tier medium-criticality UC for tests
dataSources: index=network sourcetype=cisco:asa action=blocked from Splunk_TA_cisco-asa on perimeter firewalls.
app: Splunk Add-on for Cisco ASA (Splunkbase 2847), 8.x on syslog collectors.
spl:
index=network sourcetype=cisco:asa action=blocked dest_port=443 earliest=-1h
| stats count AS blocks BY src_ip
| where blocks > 100
| sort - blocks
description: Highlights internal sources that triggered more than one hundred blocked HTTPS connection attempts in an hour on Cisco ASA firewalls.
value: Repeated blocks may indicate malware beaconing or misconfigured applications. Medium-severity visibility helps network teams tune rules without paging on every single drop.
grandmaExplanation: We count how often inside computers get turned away when they try to reach blocked websites. When one computer tries too many times, we let the network team know to take a look.
implementation: Point ASA syslog to Splunk HF, confirm sourcetype=cisco:asa, schedule hourly search, review top talkers weekly.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Splunk_TA_cisco-asa on syslog receiver; ASA sending code 106023 deny messages.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify `action=blocked` and `dest_port` fields extract via TA props.conf.

Step 2 — Create the search
Stats by src_ip; threshold 100/hour is tunable for branch offices.

Step 3 — Validate
Generate test deny from lab host; confirm increment in Splunk within 60 seconds.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Weekly email digest to network ops; no automatic paging at medium criticality.
knownFalsePositives: Patch servers and NTP misconfigurations can cause repeated denies; enrich with asset lookup.
